evidence: WebFetch target is https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vercel-labs/web-interface-guidelines/main/command.md, ref is the mutable main branch, and the fetched content is used as both the rule set and the output-format instructions
description: Skill fetches its entire ruleset and output format from a mutable main branch ref on every invocation instead of a pinned commit or tag.
suggested_fix: Pin the fetch URL to a specific commit SHA or release tag of vercel-labs/web-interface-guidelines.

evidence: WebFetch target is https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vercel-labs/writing-guidelines/main/command.md, ref is the mutable main branch, and the fetched content is used as both the rule set and the output-format instructions
description: Skill fetches its entire ruleset and output format from a mutable main branch ref on every invocation instead of a pinned commit or tag.
suggested_fix: Pin the fetch URL to a specific commit SHA or release tag of vercel-labs/writing-guidelines.

evidence: curl -s -X POST "$DEPLOY_ENDPOINT" -F "file=@$TARBALL" -F "framework=$FRAMEWORK" uploads the packaged project to https://claude-skills-deploy.vercel.com/api/deploy
description: Uploads a tarball of the project to an external deploy endpoint; this is the script's documented purpose and the tarball already excludes node_modules/.git/.env before upload.

evidence: curl -s -X POST "$DEPLOY_ENDPOINT" -F "file=@$TARBALL" -F "framework=$FRAMEWORK" uploads the packaged project to https://codex-deploy-skills.vercel.sh/api/deploy
description: Uploads a tarball of the project to an external deploy endpoint; this is the script's documented purpose and the tarball already excludes node_modules/.git/.env before upload.
Skill performs deploy/env/domain actions but has no explicit output/response-format section, unlike sibling deploy-to-vercel/SKILL.md which has a dedicated ## Output section.
suggested_fix: Add an Output section describing what to tell the user after deploy, env, and domain operations (e.g. deployment URL, confirmation of env var changes).

evidence: skills/react-native-skills/rules/ has 36 non-template files; SKILL.md Quick Reference table lists 33 rule names
description: 3 rule files exist on disk but are not listed in SKILL.md's Quick Reference: design-system-compound-components.md, scroll-position-no-state.md, state-ground-truth.md.
suggested_fix: Add the 3 missing rules to the Quick Reference table under an appropriate category (or remove them if superseded).

evidence: skills.sh.json groupings list 8 skill names across React/Vercel/Design categories; skills/ contains 9 skill directories, and writing-guidelines is absent from every grouping
description: writing-guidelines is a complete, valid skill (has its own SKILL.md matching sibling conventions) but is missing from skills.sh.json, so it won't surface in marketplace/skills.sh browsing.
suggested_fix: Add writing-guidelines to the "Design" grouping in skills.sh.json alongside web-design-guidelines.
